rawHead:
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Lie algebras</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false,
      macros:{
        '\\Spec':'\\Spec',
        '\\Gal':'\\Gal',
        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',
        '\\tr':'\\tr',
        '\\ad':'\\ad',
        '\\ind':'\\ind'
      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  .matgrid{display:inline-grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(2,minmax(74px,1fr));gap:4px;
    font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem}
  .matgrid > div{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:4px;
    padding:.35rem .45rem;text-align:right;color:#cbd5e1}
  .gallery{display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(150px,1fr));gap:.8rem;margin:.8rem 0}
  .gallery .card{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;padding:.5rem;text-align:center}
  .gallery .card .lbl{color:var(--mute);font-size:.82rem;margin-top:.25rem}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>
<!-- breadcrumb-head-auto-begin -->

hott"},"upper-half-plane-hyperbolic":{"next":"modular-forms","prev":null,"section":"Modular forms & L-functions","title":"Upper half-plane"},"vertex-operator-algebras":{"next":null,"prev":"modular-curves","section":"Modular forms & L-functions","title":"Vertex operator rawBodyPrefix:
← Notebook
1 Axioms 2 Adjoint 3 Solvable 4 Cartan–Killing 5 sl₂ & weights 6 Classification

Lie algebras

Differentiate a Lie group at the identity and you get a vector space with a bracket. The whole zoo of classical and exceptional symmetries — A, B, C, D, plus G₂, F₄, E₆, E₇, E₈ — is decoded from skew-symmetry, Jacobi, and a single bilinear form.

blocks.html:
1. Axioms and matrix examples

A Lie algebra over $\mathbb{C}$ (or any field) is a vector space $\mathfrak{g}$ together with a bilinear bracket $[\cdot,\cdot]\colon\mathfrak{g}\times\mathfrak{g}\to\mathfrak{g}$ satisfying

$$[x,x]=0\quad\text{(skew-symmetry)},\qquad [x,[y,z]]+[y,[z,x]]+[z,[x,y]]=0\quad\text{(Jacobi)}.$$

The skew condition $[x,x]=0$ is equivalent to $[x,y]=-[y,x]$ in characteristic $\neq 2$. Every associative algebra $A$ becomes a Lie algebra under the commutator bracket $[a,b]=ab-ba$; this is where every example below comes from.

Classical matrix Lie algebras

AlgebraDefining equation$\dim$Lie group
$\mathfrak{gl}_n$(all $n\times n$ matrices)$n^2$$\mathrm{GL}_n$
$\mathfrak{sl}_n$$\tr\,X = 0$$n^2-1$$\mathrm{SL}_n$
$\mathfrak{so}_n$$X+X^\top = 0$$\binom{n}{2}$$\mathrm{SO}_n$
$\mathfrak{sp}_{2n}$$X^\top J + JX = 0$, $J$ symplectic form$n(2n+1)$$\mathrm{Sp}_{2n}$

Each defining condition is preserved by $[X,Y]=XY-YX$: differentiating the group equation $g\in G$ at $g=I$ along $g(t)=I+tX+\cdots$ gives the linear equation cutting out $\mathfrak{g}\subset\mathfrak{gl}_n$.

Bracket-table inspector — $\mathfrak{sl}_2$
click a cell to see $[X,Y]$ for the standard basis $\{e,h,f\}$
Click a cell.

$e=\bigl(\begin{smallmatrix}0&1\\0&0\end{smallmatrix}\bigr)$, $h=\bigl(\begin{smallmatrix}1&0\\0&-1\end{smallmatrix}\bigr)$, $f=\bigl(\begin{smallmatrix}0&0\\1&0\end{smallmatrix}\bigr)$. Verify Jacobi by clicking the diagonal pairs.
Skew-symmetry is the antisymmetric part of the commutator; the Jacobi identity is the trace of associativity that survives. Together they are the only two axioms.

See also
  • Groups, rings, and fields
  • Lie groups and matrix examples

Used in
Lie algebras · Derivations and the adjoint representation
Quaternions, octonions, and division algebras · Unit quaternions and $\mathrm{SU}(2)\to\mathrm{SO}(3)$
</section>

2. Derivations and the adjoint representation

For each $x\in\mathfrak{g}$ define $\ad(x)\colon\mathfrak{g}\to\mathfrak{g}$ by $\ad(x)(y)=[x,y]$. The Jacobi identity says exactly that $\ad(x)$ is a derivation of the bracket:

$$\ad(x)[y,z]=[\ad(x)y,\,z]+[y,\,\ad(x)z].$$

Equivalently, $[\ad(x),\ad(y)]=\ad([x,y])$, so $\ad\colon\mathfrak{g}\to\mathfrak{gl}(\mathfrak{g})$ is itself a Lie algebra homomorphism — the adjoint representation.

The Killing form

The trace form on the adjoint rep,

$$B(x,y)=\tr\bigl(\ad(x)\,\ad(y)\bigr),$$

is symmetric, bilinear, and $\mathfrak{g}$-invariant: $B([z,x],y)+B(x,[z,y])=0$. It is canonical — the only such form up to scalar on a simple Lie algebra.

Adjoint action on $\mathfrak{sl}_2$
choose $x\in\{e,h,f\}$ and watch where $\ad(x)$ sends each basis vector
eigenvalues of $\ad(x)$:

When $x=h$, the action is diagonal with eigenvalues $\{2,0,-2\}$ — this is the root decomposition in miniature: $\mathfrak{sl}_2 = \mathfrak{g}_{-2}\oplus\mathfrak{g}_0\oplus\mathfrak{g}_{2}$.
blocks.script:
<script>
(function(){
  // ad(x) acting on basis (e,h,f). columns of M are ad(x)(e), ad(x)(h), ad(x)(f).
  const AD = {
    e: [[0,-2,0],[0,0,1],[0,0,0]],   // ad(e): e->0, h->-2e, f->h
    h: [[2,0,0],[0,0,0],[0,0,-2]],   // ad(h): e->2e, h->0, f->-2f
    f: [[0,0,0],[0,2,0],[-1,0,0]]    // ad(f): e->h, h->2f, f->0
  };
  const root = document.querySelector("#adjoint-vis");
  if(!root) return;
  const sel = root.querySelector("#adj-x");
  const eig = root.querySelector("#adj-eig");
  const svg = root.querySelector("#adj-svg");
  const positions = { e:[ 110,-60], h:[0,80], f:[-110,-60] };
  function fmtVec(v){
    const labels=["e","h","f"];
    const parts=[];
    v.forEach((c,i)=>{
      if(Math.abs(c)<1e-9) return;
      const sign = c<0 ? "-" : (parts.length? "+" : "");
      const mag = Math.abs(c)===1 ? "" : Math.abs(c)+"·";
      parts.push(sign + mag + labels[i]);
    });
    return parts.length ? parts.join(" ") : "0";
  }
  function eigsForX(x){
    if(x==="h") return "2, 0, -2";
    if(x==="e" || x==="f") return "0, 0, 0 (nilpotent)";
    return "";
  }
  function redraw(){
    const x = sel.value;
    const M = AD[x];
    eig.textContent = eigsForX(x);
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    // basis nodes
    Object.entries(positions).forEach(([lbl,p])=>{
      drawNode(svg, p[0], p[1], lbl, {r:20, stroke: "var(--yellow)"});
    });
    // for each column j, image is sum_i M[i][j] * basis_i
    const labels = ["e","h","f"];
    labels.forEach((src,j)=>{
      const col = [M[0][j], M[1][j], M[2][j]];
      const img = fmtVec(col);
      // pick a target: if image is single-vector with coefficient, draw arrow there;
      // otherwise label the source node with the formula
      const nonzero = col.map((c,i)=>[c,i]).filter(([c])=>Math.abs(c)>1e-9);
      const srcP = positions[src];
      if(nonzero.length === 1){
        const [coef,idx] = nonzero[0];
        const tgt = labels[idx];
        const tgtP = positions[tgt];
        const lbl = (coef===1?"":(coef===-1?"-":coef+"·")) + tgt;
        if(src===tgt){
          // self-loop: small label by node
          const t = SVG("text",{x:srcP[0]+28,y:srcP[1]-22,fill:"var(--mute)","font-size":12,"font-style":"italic"});
          t.textContent = "ad("+x+")("+src+")="+img;
          svg.appendChild(t);
        } else {
          drawArrow(svg, srcP, tgtP, {color:"var(--cyan)", label:lbl, markerId:"arr-adj", curve:14});
        }
      } else if(nonzero.length === 0){
        const t = SVG("text",{x:srcP[0]+28,y:srcP[1]-22,fill:"var(--mute)","font-size":12,"font-style":"italic"});
        t.textContent = "ad("+x+")("+src+")=0";
        svg.appendChild(t);
      }
    });
    // header
    const hdr = SVG("text",{x:0,y:-90,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":13,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    hdr.textContent = "ad("+x+") on the basis (e,h,f)";
    svg.appendChild(hdr);
  }
  sel.addEventListener("change", redraw);
  redraw();
})();
</script>

blocks.html: $\ad(h)$ is diagonalizable; $\ad(e)$ and $\ad(f)$ are nilpotent. The combination "diagonalizable + nilpotent" is exactly the Jordan decomposition that makes semisimple Lie algebras tractable.

See also
  • Bracket, commutators, and BCH

Used in
Lie algebras · Cartan, Killing, and semisimplicity
Lie algebras · Solvable and nilpotent Lie algebras
</section>

3. Solvable and nilpotent Lie algebras

Two filtrations of $\mathfrak{g}$ measure how far it is from being abelian:

  • The derived series $\mathfrak{g}^{(0)}=\mathfrak{g}$, $\mathfrak{g}^{(k+1)}=[\mathfrak{g}^{(k)},\mathfrak{g}^{(k)}]$. $\mathfrak{g}$ is solvable if some $\mathfrak{g}^{(k)}=0$.
  • The lower central series $\mathfrak{g}^{0}=\mathfrak{g}$, $\mathfrak{g}^{k+1}=[\mathfrak{g},\mathfrak{g}^{k}]$. $\mathfrak{g}$ is nilpotent if some $\mathfrak{g}^{k}=0$.

Nilpotent $\Rightarrow$ solvable. Two structure theorems pin them down:

  • Engel. $\mathfrak{g}$ is nilpotent $\iff$ each $\ad(x)$ is a nilpotent operator.
  • Lie. Every finite-dimensional rep of a solvable Lie algebra over $\mathbb{C}$ is simultaneously upper-triangularizable.

Derived-series collapse
step through $\mathfrak{g}^{(0)}\supseteq\mathfrak{g}^{(1)}\supseteq\cdots$ for three sample algebras
Pick an algebra and press step.
blocks.script:
<script>
(function(){
  // for each algebra: list of dims of g^{(0)}, g^{(1)}, g^{(2)}, ...
  const data = {
    b2:   { name:"α·E11+β·E22+γ·E12 (Borel)", series:[3,1,0], verdict:"solvable, NOT nilpotent" },
    heis: { name:"Heisenberg n_3", series:[3,1,0], verdict:"nilpotent (hence solvable)" },
    sl2:  { name:"sl_2", series:[3,3,3,3], verdict:"semisimple — derived series stays at full dim" }
  };
  const root = document.querySelector("#derived-series");
  if(!root) return;
  const sel = root.querySelector("#ds-which");
  const step = root.querySelector("#ds-step");
  const reset = root.querySelector("#ds-reset");
  const svg = root.querySelector("#ds-svg");
  const out = root.querySelector("#ds-out");
  let k = 0;
  function redraw(){
    const d = data[sel.value];
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const xs = [40, 140, 240, 340, 440];
    const seriesCap = Math.min(d.series.length, xs.length);
    const maxBar = 100;
    const dim0 = d.series[0];
    for(let i=0;i<seriesCap;i++){
      const x = xs[i];
      const dim = d.series[i];
      const filled = i <= k;
      const h = (dim/dim0)*maxBar + 4;
      const color = dim===0 ? "var(--green)" : (filled ? "var(--yellow)" : "var(--mute)");
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:x-26,y:130-h,width:52,height:h,
        fill: filled? color : "var(--panel2)", stroke: color, "stroke-width":1.5, rx:4}));
      const lbl = SVG("text",{x:x,y:152,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12, fill:"var(--mute)"});
      lbl.textContent = "g^("+i+")";
      svg.appendChild(lbl);
      const dlbl = SVG("text",{x:x,y:170,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12, fill:filled? "var(--ink)":"var(--mute)"});
      dlbl.textContent = "dim "+dim;
      svg.appendChild(dlbl);
    }
    const arrowY = 90;
    for(let i=0;i<seriesCap-1;i++){
      drawArrow(svg, [xs[i]+28, arrowY], [xs[i+1]-28, arrowY], {
        color: i<k ? "var(--cyan)" : "var(--mute)",
        markerId: "arr-ds-"+i, width:1.2
      });
      const t = SVG("text",{x:(xs[i]+xs[i+1])/2, y:arrowY-8, "text-anchor":"middle", "font-size":11,
        fill:i<k? "var(--cyan)":"var(--mute)", "font-style":"italic"});
      t.textContent = "[·,·]";
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    const dimNow = d.series[Math.min(k, d.series.length-1)];
    out.textContent = d.name + "\n  step k = " + k + ", dim g^(" + k + ") = " + dimNow + (dimNow===0 ? "  ⇒  series terminated" : "") + "\n  verdict: " + d.verdict;
  }
  step.addEventListener("click", ()=>{ const cap = data[sel.value].series.length-1; if(k<cap) k++; redraw(); });
  reset.addEventListener("click", ()=>{ k=0; redraw(); });
  sel.addEventListener("change", ()=>{ k=0; redraw(); });
  redraw();
})();
</script>

blocks.html: $\mathfrak{sl}_2$ is its own derived algebra — $[\mathfrak{sl}_2,\mathfrak{sl}_2]=\mathfrak{sl}_2$ — so the derived series never collapses. This is the cleanest signal of semisimplicity.

See also
  • Quotients and finite fields

Used in
Lie algebras · Cartan, Killing, and semisimplicity
</section>

4. Cartan, Killing, and the root decomposition

A Lie algebra is semisimple if its largest solvable ideal (the radical) is zero. Cartan's criterion converts this into a linear-algebra check on the Killing form:

$$\mathfrak{g}\text{ semisimple}\ \iff\ B\text{ is nondegenerate}.$$

Now pick a Cartan subalgebra $\mathfrak{h}\subset\mathfrak{g}$ — a maximal toral (ad-diagonalizable) subalgebra. The adjoint action of $\mathfrak{h}$ decomposes $\mathfrak{g}$ into simultaneous eigenspaces:

$$\mathfrak{g} = \mathfrak{h}\ \oplus\ \bigoplus_{\alpha\in\Phi}\mathfrak{g}_\alpha,\qquad \mathfrak{g}_\alpha=\{x\in\mathfrak{g}:[h,x]=\alpha(h)x\ \forall h\in\mathfrak{h}\}.$$

The nonzero linear functionals $\alpha\in\mathfrak{h}^*$ that show up form a finite set $\Phi\subset\mathfrak{h}^*$ — the root system. It is symmetric ($\Phi=-\Phi$), spans $\mathfrak{h}^*$, and satisfies the integrality conditions that make it one of the rank-$n$ root systems.

Rank-2 root systems
switch among $A_2$, $B_2$, $G_2$ — the three irreducible options in dimension $2$
blocks.script:
<script>
(function(){
  function rotate(p, ang){ const c=Math.cos(ang), s=Math.sin(ang); return [p[0]*c - p[1]*s, p[0]*s + p[1]*c]; }
  // root-system root vectors in 2D, scaled for the viewBox
  function rootsA2(){
    const r = 110;
    const out=[];
    for(let k=0;k<6;k++){
      out.push([r*Math.cos(k*Math.PI/3), r*Math.sin(k*Math.PI/3)]);
    }
    return out;
  }
  function rootsB2(){
    // 4 short (length 1), 4 long (length sqrt 2). Show short along axes, long on diagonals.
    const s = 90, L = 90*Math.SQRT2;
    return [
      [s,0],[-s,0],[0,s],[0,-s],
      [L*Math.cos(Math.PI/4), L*Math.sin(Math.PI/4)],
      [L*Math.cos(3*Math.PI/4), L*Math.sin(3*Math.PI/4)],
      [L*Math.cos(-Math.PI/4), L*Math.sin(-Math.PI/4)],
      [L*Math.cos(-3*Math.PI/4), L*Math.sin(-3*Math.PI/4)]
    ];
  }
  function rootsG2(){
    // 6 short of length 1, 6 long of length sqrt(3); short at 0,60,..., long at 30,90,...
    const s = 70, L = 70*Math.sqrt(3);
    const out=[];
    for(let k=0;k<6;k++){ out.push([s*Math.cos(k*Math.PI/3), s*Math.sin(k*Math.PI/3)]); }
    for(let k=0;k<6;k++){ out.push([L*Math.cos(Math.PI/6 + k*Math.PI/3), L*Math.sin(Math.PI/6 + k*Math.PI/3)]); }
    return out;
  }
  const data = {
    A2: { roots: rootsA2(), info:"6 roots, all the same length, hexagonal symmetry; rank 2; algebra sl_3 (8-dim)." },
    B2: { roots: rootsB2(), info:"8 roots in two orbits (short/long, ratio 1:sqrt 2); rank 2; algebra so_5 = sp_4 (10-dim)." },
    G2: { roots: rootsG2(), info:"12 roots in two orbits (short/long, ratio 1:sqrt 3); rank 2; exceptional algebra G_2 (14-dim)." }
  };
  const root = document.querySelector("#root-vis");
  if(!root) return;
  const svg = root.querySelector("#rv-svg");
  const out = root.querySelector("#rv-out");
  let cur = "A2";
  function redraw(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:-150,y1:0,x2:150,y2:0,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:0,y1:-150,x2:0,y2:150,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
    // grid circles to highlight orbits
    [40,70,90,110,130].forEach(r => {
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:0,cy:0,r:r,fill:"none",stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":0.4,"stroke-dasharray":"2 4"}));
    });
    const d = data[cur];
    d.roots.forEach((p,i)=>{
      const len = Math.hypot(p[0],p[1]);
      const isShort = (cur==="B2" && Math.abs(len-90)<2) || (cur==="G2" && Math.abs(len-70)<2);
      const color = isShort ? "var(--cyan)" : "var(--yellow)";
      // arrow from origin (note: SVG y is inverted, but our roots are symmetric so it doesn't matter visually)
      drawArrow(svg, [0,0], [p[0],p[1]], {color, markerId:"arr-rs-"+(isShort?"s":"l"), width:1.6});
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:p[0],cy:p[1],r:3.5,fill:color}));
    });
    out.textContent = cur + " — " + d.info;
  }
  root.querySelectorAll("button[data-rs]").forEach(b => {
    b.addEventListener("click", ()=>{
      cur = b.getAttribute("data-rs");
      root.querySelectorAll("button[data-rs]").forEach(x => x.classList.remove("active"));
      b.classList.add("active");
      redraw();
    });
  });
  redraw();
})();
</script>

blocks.html: A simple Lie algebra has an irreducible root system, encoded by a connected Dynkin diagram. We meet the diagrams in §6.

Used in
Lie algebras · $\mathfrak{sl}_2$-triples, root spaces, weights
</section>

5. $\mathfrak{sl}_2$-triples and weight diagrams

Every root $\alpha\in\Phi$ of a semisimple $\mathfrak{g}$ produces an embedded copy of $\mathfrak{sl}_2$:

$$e_\alpha\in\mathfrak{g}_\alpha,\quad f_\alpha\in\mathfrak{g}_{-\alpha},\quad h_\alpha=[e_\alpha,f_\alpha]\in\mathfrak{h},\qquad [h_\alpha,e_\alpha]=2e_\alpha,\ [h_\alpha,f_\alpha]=-2f_\alpha.$$

This is the $\mathfrak{sl}_2$-triple attached to $\alpha$. The classification of finite-dimensional representations of $\mathfrak{sl}_2$ is the engine driving everything that follows.

Weight diagrams of $V_n$

For each integer $n\ge 0$ there is a unique (up to iso) irreducible representation $V_n$ of $\mathfrak{sl}_2(\mathbb{C})$ of dimension $n+1$. Its weights — the eigenvalues of $h$ — are

$$n,\ n-2,\ n-4,\ \ldots,\ -n+2,\ -n,$$

each with multiplicity $1$. The action of $e$ raises by $2$, the action of $f$ lowers by $2$.

Weight diagram of $V_n$
drag the slider; click a weight to see the $e/f$ action

$\dim V_n = n+1$. Casimir $C=ef+fe+\tfrac12 h^2$ acts on $V_n$ as the scalar $\tfrac{n(n+2)}{2}$ — the analogue of $j(j+1)$ in physics with $j=n/2$.
blocks.script:
<script>
(function(){
  const root = document.querySelector("#weight-diagram");
  if(!root) return;
  const sl = root.querySelector("#wd-n");
  const info = root.querySelector("#wd-info");
  const svg = root.querySelector("#wd-svg");
  let selectedW = null;
  function redraw(){
    const n = +sl.value;
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    // axis
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:-200,y1:0,x2:200,y2:0,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
    const weights = [];
    for(let w=n; w>=-n; w-=2) weights.push(w);
    const N = weights.length;
    const span = Math.max(2, n);
    const xs = w => (w/span) * 180;
    weights.forEach(w => {
      const x = xs(w);
      const color = (w===selectedW) ? "var(--pink)" : "var(--yellow)";
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:x,cy:0,r:8,fill:color,stroke:"#fff","stroke-width":1.2,
        style:"cursor:pointer", "data-w":w}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:x,y:30,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = w;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    });
    // axis label
    const lbl = SVG("text",{x:0,y:-32,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":13,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    lbl.textContent = "weights of V_" + n + "  (h-eigenvalues)";
    svg.appendChild(lbl);
    // edges for selected
    if(selectedW !== null && Math.abs(selectedW)<=n){
      const x0 = xs(selectedW);
      if(selectedW + 2 <= n){
        drawArrow(svg, [x0+9,-12], [xs(selectedW+2)-9, -12], {color:"var(--cyan)", label:"e", markerId:"arr-wd-e"});
      }
      if(selectedW - 2 >= -n){
        drawArrow(svg, [x0-9, 12], [xs(selectedW-2)+9, 12], {color:"var(--violet)", label:"f", markerId:"arr-wd-f"});
      }
    }
    // attach click handlers
    [...svg.querySelectorAll("circle[data-w]")].forEach(c => {
      c.addEventListener("click", ev => {
        selectedW = +ev.target.getAttribute("data-w");
        redraw();
      });
    });
    if(selectedW !== null && (selectedW > n || selectedW < -n)) selectedW = null;
    if(selectedW === null){
      info.textContent = "dim V_" + n + " = " + (n+1) + ".  Casimir acts as " + ((n*(n+2))/2) + ".";
    } else {
      const eDest = (selectedW+2 <= n) ? "v_"+(selectedW+2) : "0 (top of ladder)";
      const fDest = (selectedW-2 >= -n) ? "v_"+(selectedW-2) : "0 (bottom of ladder)";
      info.textContent = "weight " + selectedW + ":  e·v -> " + eDest + ",  f·v -> " + fDest;
    }
  }
  sl.addEventListener("input", ()=>{ selectedW=null; redraw(); });
  redraw();
})();
</script>

blocks.html: In a general semisimple $\mathfrak{g}$, the simultaneous $\mathfrak{h}$-eigenspaces of an arbitrary representation $V$ are its weight spaces; restricting along each $\mathfrak{sl}_2$-triple recovers an $\mathfrak{sl}_2$-weight string. The combinatorics of root systems plus this $\mathfrak{sl}_2$ rule is enough to classify all irreducible reps.

See also
  • Highest-weight vectors for $\mathfrak{sl}_2$

Used in
Lie algebras · Classification of simple Lie algebras
</section>

6. Classification: A, B, C, D, E, F, G

Cartan and Killing's miraculous theorem: every simple complex Lie algebra is one of the four infinite families

$$A_n=\mathfrak{sl}_{n+1},\quad B_n=\mathfrak{so}_{2n+1},\quad C_n=\mathfrak{sp}_{2n},\quad D_n=\mathfrak{so}_{2n}\ (n\ge 2)$$

or one of the five exceptions $G_2,\ F_4,\ E_6,\ E_7,\ E_8$. The combinatorial fingerprint is the connected Dynkin diagram: nodes are simple roots, bonds encode angles ($1$ bond $= 120^\circ$ equal length, $2$ bonds $=135^\circ$ length-ratio $\sqrt{2}$, $3$ bonds $=150^\circ$ length-ratio $\sqrt{3}$), and the arrow on a multiple bond points from long to short root.

Dynkin-diagram gallery
classical series + the five exceptions
Click a diagram for details.
blocks.script:
<script>
(function(){
  function diagram(svgId, kind){
    // Returns an SVG element with the Dynkin diagram drawn.
    const svg = SVG("svg",{viewBox:"0 0 200 60", width:"100%", height:"60"});
    function node(x,filled){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:x,cy:30,r:6,fill:filled?"var(--yellow)":"var(--panel2)",stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":1.4}));
    }
    function bond(x1,x2,n,arrow){
      // n in {1,2,3}; arrow optional ('->' from x1 to x2 or '<-' opposite). draw n parallel lines.
      const offsets = n===1?[0]:(n===2?[-3,3]:[-5,0,5]);
      offsets.forEach(o => svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:x1+6,y1:30+o,x2:x2-6,y2:30+o,stroke:"var(--ink)","stroke-width":1.2})));
      if(n>=2 && arrow){
        const mid = (x1+x2)/2;
        const dir = arrow==="->" ? 1 : -1;
        const tri = SVG("path",{
          d: "M"+(mid - dir*4)+",26 L"+(mid + dir*4)+",30 L"+(mid - dir*4)+",34 z",
          fill:"var(--ink)"
        });
        svg.appendChild(tri);
      }
    }
    function label(t,x){
      const e = SVG("text",{x:x,y:55,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      e.textContent = t;
      svg.appendChild(e);
    }
    if(kind==="An"){
      const xs=[30,70,110,130,170];
      xs.forEach(x=>node(x,true));
      bond(xs[0],xs[1],1); bond(xs[1],xs[2],1);
      // gap shown by ellipsis
      const e = SVG("text",{x:120,y:33,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":13,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      e.textContent = "···"; svg.appendChild(e);
      bond(xs[3],xs[4],1);
      label("A_n  (n nodes)", 100);
    }
    else if(kind==="Bn"){
      const xs=[30,70,110,150,190];
      xs.forEach(x=>node(x,true));
      bond(xs[0],xs[1],1); bond(xs[1],xs[2],1);
      const e = SVG("text",{x:130,y:33,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":13,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      e.textContent = "···"; svg.appendChild(e);
      bond(xs[3],xs[4],2,"->");
      label("B_n  (last bond double, long→short)", 100);
    }
    else if(kind==="Cn"){
      const xs=[30,70,110,150,190];
      xs.forEach(x=>node(x,true));
      bond(xs[0],xs[1],1); bond(xs[1],xs[2],1);
      const e = SVG("text",{x:130,y:33,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":13,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      e.textContent = "···"; svg.appendChild(e);
      bond(xs[3],xs[4],2,"<-");
      label("C_n  (last bond double, short→long)", 100);
    }
    else if(kind==="Dn"){
      const xs=[30,70,110,140];
      xs.forEach(x=>node(x,true));
      // branch
      node(160, true);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:140+6,y1:30,x2:160-6,y2:30-12,stroke:"var(--ink)","stroke-width":1.2}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:160,cy:18,r:6,fill:"var(--yellow)",stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":1.4}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:140+6,y1:30,x2:160-6,y2:30+12,stroke:"var(--ink)","stroke-width":1.2}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:160,cy:42,r:6,fill:"var(--yellow)",stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":1.4}));
      bond(xs[0],xs[1],1); bond(xs[1],xs[2],1);
      const e = SVG("text",{x:125,y:33,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":13,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      e.textContent = "···"; svg.appendChild(e);
      label("D_n  (forked tail)", 100);
    }
    else if(kind==="G2"){
      node(70,true); node(130,true); bond(70,130,3,"->");
      label("G_2 (triple bond, long→short)", 100);
    }
    else if(kind==="F4"){
      node(40,true); node(80,true); node(120,true); node(160,true);
      bond(40,80,1); bond(80,120,2,"->"); bond(120,160,1);
      label("F_4 (middle double bond)", 100);
    }
    else if(kind==="E6"){
      node(30,true); node(60,true); node(90,true); node(120,true); node(150,true);
      bond(30,60,1); bond(60,90,1); bond(90,120,1); bond(120,150,1);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:90,y1:30-6,x2:90,y2:30-18,stroke:"var(--ink)","stroke-width":1.2}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:90,cy:30-24,r:6,fill:"var(--yellow)",stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":1.4}));
      label("E_6 (one branch off node 3 of 5)", 100);
    }
    else if(kind==="E7"){
      const xs=[20,50,80,110,140,170];
      xs.forEach(x=>node(x,true));
      bond(xs[0],xs[1],1); bond(xs[1],xs[2],1); bond(xs[2],xs[3],1); bond(xs[3],xs[4],1); bond(xs[4],xs[5],1);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:80,y1:30-6,x2:80,y2:30-18,stroke:"var(--ink)","stroke-width":1.2}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:80,cy:30-24,r:6,fill:"var(--yellow)",stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":1.4}));
      label("E_7 (one branch off node 3 of 6)", 100);
    }
    else if(kind==="E8"){
      const xs=[10,40,70,100,130,160,190];
      xs.forEach(x=>node(x,true));
      bond(xs[0],xs[1],1); bond(xs[1],xs[2],1); bond(xs[2],xs[3],1); bond(xs[3],xs[4],1); bond(xs[4],xs[5],1); bond(xs[5],xs[6],1);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:70,y1:30-6,x2:70,y2:30-18,stroke:"var(--ink)","stroke-width":1.2}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:70,cy:30-24,r:6,fill:"var(--yellow)",stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":1.4}));
      label("E_8 (one branch off node 3 of 7)", 100);
    }
    return svg;
  }
  const info = {
    An:"A_n = sl_{n+1}: dim n(n+2). Reps indexed by Young diagrams. Self-dual only when n=1.",
    Bn:"B_n = so_{2n+1}: dim n(2n+1). Spin double cover Spin_{2n+1}. Vector rep = (2n+1)-dim.",
    Cn:"C_n = sp_{2n}: dim n(2n+1). Standard rep is symplectic, dimension 2n.",
    Dn:"D_n = so_{2n}: dim n(2n-1). Two half-spin reps; D_2 = A_1 × A_1, D_3 = A_3 (low-rank coincidences).",
    G2:"G_2: dim 14. Automorphism group of the octonions; smallest exceptional algebra.",
    F4:"F_4: dim 52. Automorphisms of the exceptional Jordan algebra h_3(O).",
    E6:"E_6: dim 78. Plays a role in GUTs; complexification has fundamental rep of dim 27.",
    E7:"E_7: dim 133. Smallest fundamental rep is 56-dim (closely tied to lines on a cubic surface).",
    E8:"E_8: dim 248. Self-dual lattice, equals its own adjoint rep — the largest exception."
  };
  const root = document.querySelector("#dynkin-gallery");
  if(!root) return;
  const grid = root.querySelector("#dg-grid");
  const out = root.querySelector("#dg-out");
  const order = ["An","Bn","Cn","Dn","G2","F4","E6","E7","E8"];
  order.forEach(k => {
    const card = document.createElement("div");
    card.className = "card";
    card.style.cursor = "pointer";
    card.appendChild(diagram("dg-"+k, k));
    const lbl = document.createElement("div");
    lbl.className = "lbl";
    lbl.textContent = k;
    card.appendChild(lbl);
    card.addEventListener("click", ()=>{
      out.textContent = info[k];
      grid.querySelectorAll(".card").forEach(x => x.style.outline = "");
      card.style.outline = "2px solid var(--yellow)";
    });
    grid.appendChild(card);
  });
})();
</script>

blocks.html: $E_8$ is the largest, with $248$ dimensions. Its root lattice equals its weight lattice — a unique even unimodular lattice in dimension $8$ — and it shows up everywhere from string compactifications to sphere packing.
</section>

7. Connections

The Cartan–Killing classification is one of the great theorems of 20th-century algebra: it produces a finite list (A, B, C, D plus exceptionals) that surfaces in geometry, representation theory, and number theory in completely different guises.

Lie groups

Lie groups are the global / topological counterpart; the Lie algebra is the linearisation at the identity. The exponential map and the BCH formula are the bridge in both directions.

Representation theory

Representations of semisimple Lie algebras are classified by highest weights; characters, Weyl groups, and the Peter–Weyl theorem are the main organising principles.

Algebraic groups and number theory

Reductive algebraic groups are the algebraic-geometry counterpart of compact Lie groups; group schemes are the framework. Automorphic forms attached to algebraic groups (Langlands) are the modern arithmetic application.

Geometry and physics

Differential geometry consumes Lie-algebra-valued forms (connections, curvature); differential geometry uses these. Gauge theory and conformal field theory rely on infinite-dimensional analogues (Kac–Moody, Virasoro).

Open frontiers: quantum groups and crystal bases; geometric representation theory (D-modules, perverse sheaves); the Langlands program as a representation-theoretic bridge; categorification (Khovanov, Khovanov–Lauda–Rouquier).
